stem: Select all TRUE statements about the EstimatorV2 `precision` parameter.

options.text: It specifies the target standard error of the estimated expectation value.
A larger `precision` value requests a more accurate result.
Reaching a smaller `precision` generally requires more shots (roughly as 1/precision^2).
It is supplied to `run()` under the keyword name `shots`.
If no precision is set in the PUB or in `run()`, the estimator falls back to the options (e.g. `default_precision`).

explanation.correct: `precision` is the target standard error of the expectation value. Because the standard error falls as 1/sqrt(shots), reaching a smaller precision needs roughly 1/precision^2 shots. It can be given per-PUB or as the `precision` keyword of run(); if omitted everywhere, the estimator uses the options value (default_precision, or shots/twirling settings).

distractors.B: A smaller precision value is the more accurate (and more expensive) request; a larger value is a looser target, so 'larger = more accurate' is backwards.
distractors.D: The keyword is `precision`, not `shots`; `shots` belongs to the Sampler. Passing `shots` to EstimatorV2.run() raises a TypeError.
